events_with_summaries.title: Easter Safety Sprint
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: The Easter Safety Sprint runs April 3 through 6. With holiday traffic picking up, let’s keep the store safe and ready. Do quick safety sweeps, check and restock spill stations and PPE, and review spring weather readiness on every shift. Be sure to share your participation on Workvivo with the hashtag eastersafety.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

This message includes instruction on dealing with excess pennies.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

Keep the salesfloor full and organized, clean up seasonal areas and maintain strong floral standards. Let’s finish strong with focused execution.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

Department 90: There is currently a supply shortage of Philadelphia No Bake Cheesecake. In Q3, an additional assortment will be launched which should improve the situation.

Department 90: In week 9, ricotta cheese moved from the Cultured modular to the Cheese modular.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 80: Deli Meat & Cheese
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Keep the spotlight on standards in Department 80 to drive strong Service Deli sales. Focus on the basics and maintain high standards across the department by ensuring the area is well lit, fixtures are clean, set correctly and in good working condition, items are rotated, stocked, priced and presentable, inventory is organized and on hands are accurate, and all food safety guidelines are being followed in accordance with the Better Way guide. Finally, cut markdowns on pre-sliced deli meat and cheese by labeling and stocking only what is needed to meet the customer demand and don't forget to utilize the sampling program to entice customers and drive sales.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 93: Easter Week is Here!
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Easter is here! Let’s finish the season strong in Department 93 and deliver top quality Fresh Meat to our valued customers. Focus on Easter favorites: turkeys, hams, Cornish hens, boneless rib roasts and whole tenderloins and stock bunkers strategically throughout the day to keep them full and drive sales. Hold steady on Easter hams: The Saturday before Easter is one of the biggest days of the season with the average store turning the Marketside ham bunker about five times. Avoid early markdowns on fresh turkeys: Merchants will monitor sell-through and support with post-Easter pricing, lowering the price for whole fresh turkeys to fifty cents per pound after the holiday. Finally, protect quality with meticulous temperature management by maintaining the 30 degree target temperature. At 34 degrees, shelf life starts to diminish quickly, losing one day of life for each degree the temp raises. If browning appears or leaks are visible, check the temperatures immediately. Review the time-lapse temperature video for examples.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Produce Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Harsh weather, extreme temperatures, pests and disease have impacted domestic crops in the South, Southeast and Southwest and Mexico. As a result, expect delays, low level inventory shipments and possible outs on the following items: berries (blueberries and raspberries), bok choy, Napa cabbage, carrots, cilantro, corn, cucumbers, green beans, organic lettuce, peppers (green bell peppers, jalapeño peppers, tomatillo peppers), Roma tomatoes, watermelons and other mixed vegetables. Most of these items will be spotty through week 11 (Apr. 11-17) and are projected to improve over the next few weeks. Continue to monitor inventory, fill gaps with available high-velocity items and maintain flexibility until the supply has returned to normal. Currently, corn supply is very low and will remain tight until mid-April. As a result, the timeline to transition bulk corn from the wet wall to modularized bins has shifted from week 8 to week 14. Check back here for weekly updates throughout the summer.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 98 & 81: WK10 Bakery & Bread Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Keep Fresh Bakery Easter items full through Sunday, April 5. On Monday, April 6 transition back to core assortment and set high-velocity features with strong core sellers. Consider featuring Freshness Guaranteed 10-count cookies, 8-count cinnamon rolls, and 6-inch cheesecakes and 4-count Marketside muffins. Adjust production levels down so not to overproduce and be sure to keep your presentation clean during the conversion.

Return Department 81 Commercial Bread displays back to normal on Monday, April 6. Work with DSD suppliers to adjust inventory levels down to meet everyday customer demand. Finally, be sure to keep the Brown & Serve Rolls home location full, but not overstocked.

events_with_summaries.title: AI Tip of the Week
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Keep Calm and Ask My Assistant - AI Tip of the Week

Enter a real, specific problem into My Assistant and ask for solutions. AI works better when given a real issue, not just generic questions.

Example: "Our store has long checkout lines between 5-7 p.m. and limited staff. What are five practical ways we can improve checkout flow without adding more associates?"

events_with_summaries.title: New Restroom Disinfectant and Soap Dispenser Test Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Backroom:

Restroom disinfectant is shifting from Oxivir to TB disinfectant. Orders for Oxivir have stopped, and remaining supply should be used before fully transitioning by April 15 or as inventory runs out.

The Diversey soap dispenser test has ended. Dispensers in select stores should be removed and replaced with Ecolab units. Stores should confirm their status and follow up with Ecolab if replacements are not complete. Use the lookup tool on the Weekly Message page to verify if your store was part of this test.
